Title: Heterologous expression reveals that GABA does not directly inhibit the vacuolar anion channel AtALMT9

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Despite a proposed GABA binding motif by analogy to TaALMT1, AtALMT9 shows no direct response to GABA, indicating that GABA-mediated inhibition is not a conserved property across all ALMT family members. AtALMT9 and AtALMT1 belong to different ALMT clades, suggesting that structural divergence underlies their different GABA sensitivities. The authors speculate that in vivo GABA effects on vacuolar ion transport and stomatal aperture may be mediated indirectly through unknown GABA-activated regulatory components or other vacuolar ion channels/transporters.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AtALMT9 is a vacuolar anion channel that mediates Cl- influx into the vacuole during stomatal opening.
2. Cytosolic application of GABA at 100 μM, 2 mM, or 10 mM does not inhibit AtALMT9-mediated Cl- currents and does not cause current rundown over at least 20 minutes.
3. GABA applied to the vacuolar lumen (2 mM GABA_lum) likewise has no detectable effect on AtALMT9-mediated Cl- currents.
4. GABA does not affect the activation of AtALMT9 by 1 mM cytosolic malate2-, indicating that AtALMT9 is not directly regulated by GABA from either side of the vacuolar membrane.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtALMT9 functions as a vacuolar anion channel mediating Cl- influx into the vacuole, and its ion transport activity and malate-dependent activation are not directly affected by GABA applied from either the cytosolic or vacuolar side.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GABA, applied from either the cytosolic or vacuolar side, does not directly regulate AtALMT9 channel activity or its activation by cytosolic malate2-.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Vacuolar Cl- transport associated with stomatal opening; AtALMT9 activity within this pathway is not directly regulated by GABA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ALMT (aluminum-activated malate transporter) family vacuolar anion channel.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuolar membrane (tonoplast) anion channel mediating Cl- influx into the vacuole.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Application of GABA at 100 μM, 2 mM, or 10 mM to the cytosolic side (GABA_cyt) in the presence of 100 mM cytosolic Cl- and 0 or 1 mM cytosolic malate2-; application of 2 mM GABA to the vacuolar (pipette) solution (GABA_lum).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of vacuolar anion channels by GABA; GABA signaling and stomatal aperture control in plants.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No statistically significant differences were detected in AtALMT9-mediated Cl- currents between control conditions and up to 10 mM GABA_cyt, nor between absence and presence of 2 mM GABA_lum; GABA did not alter the magnitude of malate2--activated currents, and currents showed no rundown over 20 min (n = 3–10).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Nicotiana benthamiana mesophyll vacuoles from transiently transformed leaves expressing AtALMT9-GFP; isolated vacuoles used for cytosolic-side-out patch-clamp recordings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Transient expression of AtALMT9-GFP in N. benthamiana leaves; isolation of mesophyll vacuoles; cytosolic-side-out patch-clamp with voltage pulses from positive to negative potentials; perfusion of the cytosolic side with solutions containing 100 mM BTP-Cl, CaCl2, with or without GABA and 1 mM malate2-; addition of GABA to the vacuolar (pipette) solution; recording current–voltage relationships and time courses; statistical comparison using paired and unpaired two-sample Student's t tests.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cytosolic-side-out patch-clamp electrophysiology on isolated mesophyll vacuoles from Nicotiana benthamiana leaves transiently expressing AtALMT9-GFP, testing Cl- currents in response to GABA and cytosolic malate.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously characterized vacuolar anion channel AtALMT9 was selected as a candidate GABA target; an AtALMT9-GFP construct, known to complement almt9 knockout phenotypes, was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana for functional electrophysiological testing.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Clarifies that GABA does not directly modulate the vacuolar anion channel AtALMT9, indicating that breeding or engineering strategies aiming to alter GABA-dependent stomatal behavior should target other regulators or ion transporters rather than AtALMT9 itself.
